title: Scimitar assault wing
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Youmay|the Scimitar assault [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] unit|other uses of the word [[Scimitar (disambiguation)|scimitar]]|linebreak=1}} {{MilitaryUnit |image=[[File:Scimitar-AJ15.jpg]] |name=Scimitar assault wing
"White Death" |type=Special [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Bomber/Legends|bomber]] [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]]{{JournalCite|15|Special Military Unit Intelligence Update}} |formation=[[Fighter wing/Legends|Fighter wing]]''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' |commander=[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tomax Bren/Legends|Tomax Bren]] |second= |staff= |subunit=6 [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] |strength=*3 squadrons of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] *2 squadrons of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] *1 [[Special Unit squadron]] **1 [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] of TIE/LN starfighters and [[TIE/rc starfighter]]s **2 flights of [[MT/191 drop-ship]]s |vehicles=*[[Scimitar assault bomber]]s *TIE/sa bombers *TIE/LN starfighters *TIE/rc starfighters *MT/191 drop-ships |headquarters=[[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Retribution (Imperial-class)|''Retribution'']] |location=Mobile with the [[Qeimet fleet]] |battles=*[[Siege of Nulan VI]] *[[Assault of the Aturi Cluster]] *[[Assault of U'Dray]] *[[Battle of Mrisst (9 ABY)|Battle of Mrisst]] |formed= |founded= |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|Mission success and personal survival hinge on each member of the wing doing as expected when it is expected of him. Failure to do so through negligence, poor timing, or exercise of personal initiative is inexcusable.|Captain Tomax Bren, in a [[datamemo]] to the Scimitar assault wing's squadron leaders|}} The '''Scimitar assault wing''' was an elite [[Bomber/Legends|bomber]] unit operating within the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tomax Bren/Legends|Tomax Bren]], the Scimitar assault [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]]—also known as "[[Color/Legends|White]] [[Death/Legends|Death]]" due to the light coloration of their [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]—was based off the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Retribution (Imperial-class)|''Retribution'']], and assigned to the [[Qeimet fleet]]. Comprised of six [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]], the Scimitar assault wing employed a variety of [[Starship/Legends|craft]] to achieve their mission profiles. Their primary craft was the [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bomber]], with [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and [[TIE/rc starfighter]]s providing support along with [[MT/191 drop-ship]]s for coordinated [[Ground warfare/Legends|ground assault]] landings. The unit made its reputation during the [[Hook Nebula campaign]], where Captain Bren's tactics and strategies—many of which breached [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial High Command's]] guidelines on the use of TIE bombers—resulted in several victories, and the wing was credited for playing an integral part in the elimination of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] presence in the [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]] region. The Imperial retreat following the Alliance victory at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] saw the Scimitar assault wing change mission profiles, and they were increasingly called upon to cover retreats and evacuations. Still considered an elite unit, the wing survived the post-[[Endor/Legends|Endor]] period through inaction until the return of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. Thrawn rearmed the Scimitar assault wing with the new [[Scimitar assault bomber]], a craft designed in conjunction with Captain Bren. Equipped with seventy-two of the new craft, the Scimitar assault wing was employed as a front-line asset, and was instrumental in the [[Battle of Mrisst (9 ABY)|assault]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mrisst/Legends|Mrisst]] during Thrawn's [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the Alliance's successor state, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Following Thrawn's defeat at the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|battle]] of [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi]], the Scimitar assault wing was left to languish, and Captain [[Zgorth'sth]] of the New Republic's [[Department of Threat Assessment]] postulated that Bren would soon lead the unit to defect to an Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] faction in order to see action, leading Zgorth'sth to suggest that the New Republic would be prudent to eliminate the unit at the first opportunity. ==History== ===Hook Nebula campaign=== {{Quote|Heavy losses sustained in the ongoing assault of U'Dray have put us understrength. We are receiving a replacement complement of new TIE pilots from the training squadron of the ''Victorious''.|Captain Tomax Bren, in a datamemo to the Scimitar assault wing's squadron leaders|}} {{Main|Hook Nebula campaign}} [[File:TIEbomber_negvv.png|thumb|right|The TIE/sa bomber, Scimitar assault wing's primary starfighter]] The Scimitar assault [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]] was a specialized [[Bomber/Legends|bomber]] unit serving within the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|naval forces]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Formed shortly after the introduction of the [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bomber]] into the Imperial [[Navy/Legends|Navy]], the Scimitar assault wing was created from six separate bomber [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]], initially as a temporary measure to counter the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] presence within the [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]], a strategic [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The assault wing was assigned to the [[Qeimet fleet]], and was based off the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Retribution (Imperial-class)|''Retribution'']], which was stationed near the Hook [[Nebula/Legends|Nebula]] and tasked with eradicating the Alliance presence in the region. The Alliance had taken control of key manufacturing and industrial centers on several resource-rich [[Planet/Legends|planets]] within the Hook Nebula, and were using their output to resupply their [[Alliance Military/Legends|own forces]]. In addition to driving out the Alliance forces, the [[Qeimet system/Legends|Qeimet]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] was also ordered to punish those worlds that had allowed the Alliance to establish a presence on their surface in the first place. The Scimitar assault wing was assigned a key role in the operation, which included the precision [[Proton bomb/Legends|bombing]] of enemy targets without damaging industrial and manufacturing facilities that the Empire wished to take control of for their own use. Placed under the [[Commander/Legends|command]] of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tomax Bren/Legends|Tomax Bren]], the Scimitar assault wing was the first unit of its type deployed to the Qeimet fleet. Upon assuming command, Captain Bren immediately set about changing the way the Scimitar assault wing operated. Instead of adhering to the guidelines issued by [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial High Command]] regarding the deployment and usage of [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] bombers, Bren began altering the unit's tactics, deployment formations and [[Starfighter combat/Legends|mission attack priorities]] to better suit the mission profiles and battle theaters in which the wing was assigned to operate. The changes he implemented were looked down upon by some of the wing's older [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], but they proved to be effective. During the [[Hook Nebula campaign]], Bren and the Scimitar assault wing were responsible for curtailing Alliance gains in the sector. The Scimitar assault wing's primary mission was planetary assault and low-level [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] bombing runs. While fully capable of conducting these missions in support of [[Soldier/Legends|ground troops]], the Scimitar assault wing was also capable of independent bombing missions, which were used for psychological intimidation and to destroy tactical targets. The unit was so adept at this mission profile that they could bomb a single building to [[Death/Legends|kill]] entrenched Rebel leadership, or eliminate an entire government by destroying them while in their own council chambers. Since the formation of the unit, the Scimitar assault wing performance record became the standard for Imperial planetary assault and [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] support missions. [[File:Hook_Nebula.jpg|thumb|left|The Hook Nebula, where the Scimitar assault wing made their name]] The first use of Bren's new tactics was at the [[Siege of Nulan VI|siege]] of the planet [[Nulan VI]], where the Scimitar assault wing employed deep-atmospheric precision bombing to force the surrender of the planet's Rebel-held [[Unidentified Nulan VI capital|capital]]. Later tactical innovations included the "[[Vertical Swoop]]," which was used to great effect against entrenched Rebel [[Anti-aircraft weaponry|anti-atmospheric defenses]] during the [[Assault of the Aturi Cluster|assault]] of the [[Aturi Cluster/Legends|Aturi Cluster]]. The Scimitar assault wing also participated in an [[Assault of U'Dray|assault]] on the planet [[U'Dray]]. Combat losses incurred during the ongoing campaign placed the wing understrength, forcing them to accept replacements of TIE pilots from the [[Unidentified training squadron|training squadron]] aboard the Qeimet fleet [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], [[Victorious (capital ship)|''Victorious'']]. However, due to the ongoing assault on U'Dray, the new pilots had no [[Time/Legends|time]] to undergo standard indoctrination into the wing and were required to complete their training exclusively in live-fire bomber combat missions. Despite the presence of the Scimitar assault wing, the Alliance secured victory at U'Dray,{{CSWECite|III|276|U'Dray}} although the Empire was eventually victorious in the Hook Nebula campaign, and the actions of the Scimitar assault wing were credited as a major factor in the victory. During the campaign, the assault wing earned the battle name "[[Color/Legends|White]] Death" due to the light coloration of their TIE bombers, which made them difficult to detect by ground observers during their attack runs until they had struck their target. Upon the conclusion of the Hook Nebula campaign, Captain Bren submitted several post-campaign reports regarding the deployment and composition of bomber wings, advocating a [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]]-based fleet and the need for TIE wings to be reconfigured to suit the needs of the current mission and combat theater. He also submitted recommendations for a proposed [[TIE Bomber Mark 2]] which would eliminate several flaws that he felt were present in the current generation of TIE bombers, including a less visible profile and upgrades to the [[Starship/Legends|craft's]] [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] and [[Armor/Legends|armor]]. Bren also filed reports on his new tactics, including the Vertical Swoop and the [[Coordinated Bombing-Landing]]. The reports and tactics were distributed to other TIE wings throughout the Empire: the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]]–controlled wings adopted Bren's tactics, as they often faced similar challenges during their missions, but the Navy-based wings saw little use for his innovations. Despite their record and success rate, the configuration of the Scimitar assault wing was copied by only a handful of units within the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]]. ===Mixed fortunes=== {{Quote|When, under what circumstances, and to whom the Scimitar Wing will defect remains to be seen.|Captain Zgorth'sth, New Republic Department of Threat Assessment|}} Following the Hook Nebula campaign, the Scimitar assault wing was deployed in a number of other assault missions, but none were as demanding as its previous assignment. Following the death of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' the Scimitar assault wing found itself employed in a new role. Instead of participating in invasions and ground assaults, the wing was assigned to covering evacuations and running interference as the Empire was reduced to a quarter of its size under constant pressure from the Alliance's successor state, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Considered to be too valuable to waste on anything other than crucial missions, the Scimitar assault wing survived the post-[[Endor/Legends|Endor]] period mainly through inaction. In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] returned from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] to take command of the remnants of the Empire.''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' Thrawn had read Bren's mission reports and recommendations for an upgraded version of the TIE bomber and agreed with his assessments. After consulting with Bren, Thrawn ordered the production of a new TIE model based on the Captain's recommendations and specifications. The [[Scimitar assault bomber]] was the result; Bren's earlier reports had found some purchase with the designers at [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]], who had agreed with the pilots' findings. As a result, the Scimitar had been in the planning stages for several [[Standard year/Legends|years]], and production on the new [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] began in [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]]. [[File:ScimitarBomber_egvv.jpg|thumb|right|Design schematics of the Scimitar assault bomber]] Outfitted with the new Scimitar assault bomber, the Scimitar assault wing played a crucial role in Thrawn's [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the New Republic. They were employed to strike at targets such as [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] and planetary defenses, and a full wing of seventy-two craft''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' played a vital role in the [[Battle of Mrisst (9 ABY)|assault]] on the planet [[Mrisst/Legends|Mrisst]]. However, Thrawn's death at the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]]''[[The Last Command]]'' left the Scimitar assault wing as part of Captain [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon's]] command. The wing was ignored by the resurrected [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] during [[Operation Shadow Hand]], and its members quickly found themselves as an elite unit with no mission profile. A High Command unwilling to engage the ascendant New Republic did not know how to use the wing, and it was often without adequate support. Sometime after the [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|defeat]] of the resurrected Emperor, Captain [[Zgorth'sth]] of the New Republic [[Department of Threat Assessment]] authored a report on Imperial and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] units that could potentially pose a threat to the security of the New Republic. He believed that Captain Bren would ultimately bridle under the restrictions and inactivity that his unit was forced to endure, and eventually defect to another Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|faction]] in order to be used effectively. While Zgorth'sth felt that inducing Bren to retire himself and his unit was the most ideal approach to countering the wing, he doubted that Bren would do so, as he was loyal to the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. Instead, he called for the elimination of the wing by drawing it into an assault mission and using high-speed starfighters to destroy or cripple the unit. ==Organization== ===Flight personnel=== {{Quote|Ideally, Bren should be induced to retire his unit. This is highly unlikely though as he and his men have always been highly politically reliable to the New Order.|Captain Zgorth'sth, New Republic Department of Threat Assessment|}} Under Captain Bren's guidance, the [[Officer/Legends|officers]] assigned to the Scimitar assault wing were considered to be more dedicated to the goals of the New Order than their comrades, and more [[Politics/Legends|politically]] reliable. Scimitar assault wing pilots were held to a high level of discipline due to the unit's precisely timed and coordinated bombing runs, where split-second timing and reactions were needed to ensure a successful mission. Mission timing was considered to be extremely critical, as all deployed [[Flight/Legends|elements]], [[Flight group/Legends|flights]], and squadrons were relied upon to be exactly where they were meant to be when the designated time came. Pilots were drilled that success was achieved through each member of the wing doing exactly what was required of them at the given moment. Penalties were enacted upon squadron members who failed in their duty due to their own negligence, or the application of initiative. In some cases, pilots who were deemed to deviate from the mission consistently were demoted to [[Dropship/Legends|dropship]] duty. Extremely capable and dedicated, the pilots of the wing often sought to better their own outstanding records and mission performance. As most of their missions tended to be atmosphere-based, the pilots of the Scimitar assault wing were among the most skilled TIE pilots to fly in atmosphere throughout the Imperial Military. Each pilot assigned to the wing completed fifty combat missions before rotating out to serve in a support capacity, including [[Training officer/Legends|pilot training]] and mission planning. As new pilots qualified for the support rotation, those pilots already serving in a support position returned to combat duty. Each individual pilot was trained to fly starfighters, with a speciality in piloting Scimitar assault bombers. They were also trained [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]], and could repair their craft if the need arose. Technical skills possessed by wing members including training in communications equipment and [[Computer/Legends|computer]] programming and repair. They were also trained in survival techniques, and were skilled in the use of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]]. Each pilot was issued with a [[navigation computer linkup helmet]] equipped with an internal [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], a high-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] stress [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] with [[Life support/Legends|life support]] equipment, a week's worth of emergency [[Ration pack/Legends|rations]], and a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]]. ====Captain Tomax Bren==== [[File:Tomax_Bren_SWJ15.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Captain Tomax Bren]] {{Quote|Captain Bren has always been a loyal Imperialist maverick, and is unlikely to tolerate the isolation fate has imposed on his wing.|Captain Zgorth'sth, New Republic Department of Threat Assessment|}} {{Main|Tomax Bren/Legends}} Captain Tomax Bren was the inaugural commander of the Scimitar assault wing, and presided over the unit. Described as a loyalist maverick, Bren's changes to the wing's operational duties were in direct contravention to Imperial High Command's standing guidelines regarding the deployment of TIE bombers. Only the outstanding success rate of the wing saved the Captain from censure by High Command. An investigation into Bren's attempts to change Imperial doctrine was convened and prosecuted, but ultimately the wing's successes led Bren to be vindicated and left in charge of the assault wing. However, Bren had effectively exchanged his career for the good of the assault wing, but it mattered little to him, as it allowed him to remain in command and fly in service to the Empire. His career languished, but as long as he kept securing victory for the Empire, he was politically untouchable. ===Headquarters=== {{Quote|In the Hook Nebula campaign, the ISD ''Retribution'' was devoted primarily to assist in the Scimitar's assault mission, subordinating an entire Star Destroyer to what is generally considered a support element.|Captain Zgorth'sth, New Republic Department of Threat Assessment|}} {{Main|Retribution (Imperial-class)}} The Scimitar assault wing was stationed aboard the ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer ''Retribution'', which was assigned to the Qeimet fleet during the Galactic Civil War. As the Scimitar assault wing earned a reputation for excellence and became the most well-known of the Qeimet fleet's fighter wings, their effectiveness and success led them to become the Qeimet fleet's shock unit of choice for dangerous assaults. As a result, the ''Retribution'' was subordinated to the assault wing, and was exclusively tasked with providing support to the unit which was an unorthodox move, but proof of Captain Bren's combat record. Following the Imperial defeat at Endor, the ''Retribution'' briefly served in the forces commanded by [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Lobax Resuun]] and served as his flagship and the seat of government for the [[Tamarin sector/Legends|Tamarin sector]].{{WEGCite|story=The Art of Betrayal|book=The Politics of Contraband}} ===Squadrons=== {{Quote|While risky, the wing does generally operate as a whole unit—properly laid out and executed, the entire wing might be crippled or eliminated in one stroke.|Captain Zgorth'sth, New Republic Department of Threat Assessment|}} The Scimitar assault wing was comprised of six individual squadrons. Three squadrons were equipped with the TIE/sa bomber, and were primarily employed to achieve the assault wing's main mission goals. In general, the assault wing deployed its bomber squadrons to attack strategic and tactical targets from orbit or atmosphere. While used to support ground operations, single flights or elements were routinely detached to eliminate smaller targets on the battlefield. Two squadrons flew [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and were used for escort and fighter support for the bomber squadrons. When not used to screen the bombers, the TIE fighter squadrons were employed in ground strafing missions, and to harass enemy soldiers and vehicle [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]]. The sixth squadron was known as the [[Special Unit squadron]], and was made up of [[Scout/Legends|reconnaissance craft]] and drop ships. The Special Unit squadron was divided into three flights; the first flight was comprised of equal numbers of TIE starfighters and [[TIE/rc starfighter]]s, and were deployed to gather intelligence for the assault wing using the recon craft's specialized [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] and long-range communications packages. The TIE starfighters assigned to the flight would cover the slower reconnaissance fighters when needed, but if the mission profile allowed for TIE/rcs to be unescorted, the TIE fighters were detached to assist the escort squadrons. The other two flights of the squadron were made up of [[Meller & Dax]] [[MT/191 drop-ship]]s used for the transport of ground [[Troop/Legends|troops]] during combined assaults. While not used for combat duty, the drop ships served as [[Transport/Legends|cargo vessels]] and [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]]. ===Spacecraft=== ====TIE/sa bomber==== [[File:TIEBomber-SotGSE.png|thumb|right|150px|The TIE/sa bomber]] {{Main|TIE/sa bomber/Legends}} The Scimitar assault wing operated three squadrons of TIE/sa bombers. Developed by Sienar Fleet Systems, the TIE/sa was the successor to the [[TIE/gt starfighter]],[[The Star Wars Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Star Wars Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] which was a TIE starfighter variant that was deployed as a ground-targeting starfighter and bomber.{{JournalCite|10|Alliance Intelligence Report: TIE Fighters}} Originally designed with a single elongated pod that contained the flight system and ordnance bay, Sienar soon realized that adding an extra pod would give them more room to incorporate the bomber's systems. The starboard pod housed the pilot, along with flight computers and life support, while the port pod was the bomber's ordnance bay. Measuring 7.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length, the TIE/sa was armed with two [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and could be equipped with [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]], proton bombs, [[Space mine/Legends|orbital mines]], and free-falling [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]]. ====TIE/LN starfighter==== [[File:Tiefighterfull.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The TIE/LN starfighter]] {{Main|TIE/LN starfighter}} TIE/LN starfighters were deployed in two dedicated squadrons by the Scimitar assault wing, with several more assigned to the first flight of the Special Unit squadron to act as escort for the unit's TIE/rc starfighters. Produced by Sienar Fleet Systems in staggering quantities, the TIE/LN starfighter was one of the most recognizable symbols of Imperial Military might, and several variant models such as the TIE/rc reconnaissance starfighter, [[TIE/fc starfighter|TIE/fc]] fire control starfighter, and the TIE/gt bomber were employed by the Empire. Exceptionally fast and maneuverable, the TIE/LN lacked a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and deflector shields and was armed with only two [[Laser/Legends|laser]] [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]]. Delicate and vulnerable, TIE squadrons often compensated for their deficiencies by shooting first, attacking in swarms, and accepting casualties. Nevertheless, TIE pilots were considered to be an elite force within the Imperial Navy, and were respected and feared by their opponents. ====TIE/rc starfighter==== [[File:TIErc.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The TIE/rc starfighter]] {{Main|TIE/rc starfighter}} The Scimitar assault wing deployed TIE/rc starfighters in the first flight of their Special Unit squadron alongside TIE/LN starfighters for escort. Measuring 6.3 meters, Sienar Fleet Systems' TIE/rc, also known as the TIE/reconnaissance, was a variant of the standard TIE/LN used for forward scouting during combat. The TIE/rc carried an upgraded sensor package and a long-range communications array{{DB|starship|tiercvanguard|TIE/rc vanguard}} to allow them to scout ahead of the main Imperial force and relay data and intelligence on the enemy. Lacking a hyperdrive, the TIE/rc was equipped with enhanced [[Engine/Legends|thrusters]] to allow it conduct fast reconnaissance sweeps and avoid combat, and was mounted with a single laser cannon for offensive and defensive purposes. ====MT/191 drop-ship==== [[File:IconOfADropship-DSTC.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The MT/191 drop-ship]] {{Main|MT/191 drop-ship}} Scimitar assault wing's Special Unit squadron employed two flights of Meller & Dax MT/191 drop-ships. Measuring 52.5 meters in length, the MT/191 was designed to powerdive into a planet's atmosphere before landing on the surface in a controlled manner after the freefall. Covered in an ablative coating known as [[VACX]] to increase the craft's [[Hull/Legends|hull]] rating, MT/191 drop-ships were armed with a single light laser cannon which was retracted into the body during atmospheric entry. The MT/191 was capable of transporting up to 160 passengers, and was also capable of landing on a planet normally. ====Scimitar assault bomber==== [[File:Tiescim.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The Scimitar assault bomber]] {{Main|Scimitar assault bomber}} The Scimitar assault bomber was the result of Captain Bren's recommendations for an improved TIE bomber, design work by Sienar Fleet Systems engineers who had agreed with his findings, and the return of Grand Admiral Thrawn. After consultations with Bren, Thrawn ordered the Imperial industrial centers to begin producing the Scimitar assault bomber. Measuring 13.8 meters in length, the Scimitar was powered by an ion thrust sublight engine and two interlocked [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] generators. The result was a stable, nimble atmospheric craft that was employed as a dedicated assault bomber. Armed with two laser cannons—which could be angled to conduct strafing runs—the Scimitar's main weaponry was twin racks of concussion missiles mounted under its wings.''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'' ==Behind the scenes== The Scimitar assault wing was first mentioned in the [[Imperial Sourcebook|first edition of the ''Imperial Sourcebook'']], published by [[West End Games]] in [[1989]]. In [[2000]], [[Decipher]] produced the ''[[Death Star II Limited]]'' expansion pack for the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]]. In the expansion, cards representing a [[Scimitar Squadron (Imperial)/Legends|Scimitar Squadron]] were included, designating them as an elite TIE bomber unit. However, the squadron's [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] is designated as [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Rhymer/Legends|Rhymer]], not Captain Bren.{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=Major Rhymer}} No source has linked Scimitar Squadron with the Scimitar assault wing. ==Sources== *''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'' *[[Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''Imperial Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] *''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *{{JournalCite|15|Special Military Unit Intelligence Update}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{FactFile|86|SCI 1-2|Scimitar Assault Bomber}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *[[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|Volume III, under "Qeimet fleet"}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Imperial fighter wings]] [[Category:Imperial Special Forces units]]
